_meta.status: candidate pool — PENDING HUMAN SIGN-OFF; case_ids provisional until kill-switch

candidates.revelation_source: Muddy Waters LLC 'Strong Sell' initiating-coverage report alleging inflated revenue/assets and cash diversion (muddywatersresearch.com, Nov 10 2010); stock opened ~$11, sold off and was halted
candidates.scheme_summary: Kept two conflicting sets of books and used false sales contracts to overstate US-reported revenue ~15x (~$491M vs ~$31M in Chinese books) and diverted securities-offering proceeds to buy a home, cars and clothing for the CEO/chairman couple (per SEC LR-22699 / AAER-3482)
candidates.notes: Nasdaq Global Market; Nevada shell (Jade Mountain Corp) reverse-merged with Dalian Rino Oct 2007. Data.sec.gov submissions confirm pre-revelation (before 2010-11-10) periodic reports: 3 Form 10-K (FY2007 filed 2008-04-01, FY2008 filed 2009-03-31, FY2009 filed 2010-03-31) + 8 Form 10-Q (2008 Q1-Q3, 2009 Q1-Q3, 2010 Q1-Q2). The Q3-2010 10-Q was filed 2010-11-15, AFTER the revelation, so excluded. Two additional shell-era 10-QSBs (Jade Mountain, 2007) exist but are pre-operating. All isXBRL flags = 0 (no XBRL). AAER-3482 was issued jointly with LR-22699. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] Complaint (comp-pr2013-87) para.4/24/26 confirms $491M vs $31M, 2008-01~2010-09 period, Nov 10 2010 report ('a Hong Kong-based investment firm' -- Muddy Waters not named in primary); trading halt per complaint para.13 was 2010-11-18, not the revelation day.

candidates.revelation_source: 8-K (filed 2011-02-01) disclosing that on 2011-01-28 the SEC commenced a formal civil investigation into CSC's previously-disclosed MSS/Nordic accounting adjustments (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/23082/000002308211000002/form8-k.htm)
candidates.scheme_summary: Overstated earnings and concealed losses on its UK NHS contract via improper percentage-of-completion models; Australian 'cookie jar' reserves and failure to record expenses; and Nordic misconduct including improper accounting for client disputes, overstated assets and improperly capitalized 'prepaid' costs (per SEC order 33-9804/AAER-3662 para.1, 10-11, 98).
candidates.notes: AMBIGUOUS first-revelation date. first_revelation_date set to the firmly-dated SEC formal civil investigation (2011-01-28, disclosed via 8-K filed 2011-02-01). HOWEVER CSC had already disclosed Nordic (MSS) out-of-period accounting adjustments/irregularities in its FY2011 quarterly reports beginning ~Aug 2010 (Q1 FY2011 10-Q, quarter ended 2010-07-02), which the company characterized as immaterial; a human verifier may reasonably push first_revelation into ~Aug-Nov 2010. Order text: 'From 2009 to 2011, CSC engaged in a wide-ranging accounting and disclosure fraud'; restatement covered fiscal 2009-2012. CSC settled 2015-06-05, $190M penalty. CIK 0000023082 (later CSC merged into DXC 2017). pre_revelation_quarters_available null: CSC has a very deep filing history (public filer for decades) and the form[] array could not be reliably extracted from the truncated submissions JSON. XBRL: large accelerated filer, XBRL mandatory for periods ending after 2009-06-15, so pre-revelation FY2010/FY2011 10-K and 10-Qs carry XBRL. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->68 (EDGAR exact). scheme_summary corrected: order para.1/10 attributes 'cookie jar' reserves to AUSTRALIA only; Nordic conduct = client-dispute accounting, overstated assets, capitalized 'prepaid' costs (para.11, 98) -- the prior wording conflated the two regions. $190M penalty confirmed (sec.IV undertaking R).

candidates.revelation_source: Citron Research report ('too good to be true' / 'phantom company') alleging CCME does not exist at its reported scale (citronresearch.com, Jan 30 2011); CCME fell ~14% on Jan 31 2011
candidates.scheme_summary: After a reverse merger, grossly overstated cash balances and inflated revenue/profits and falsely claimed multinational advertising clients; fraud surfaced via fabricated bank confirmations, and the CEO tried to bribe an investigating accountant (per SEC LR-22731 / AAER-3479)
candidates.notes: NASDAQ; Delaware SPAC TM Entertainment & Media reverse-merged with Hong Kong Mandefu Holdings (China MediaExpress) Oct 2009. AAER-3479 issued jointly with LR-22731 (2013-06-20); later AAER-3540 (2014-02-14) covers default judgment vs CEO Zheng Cheng. First public revelation was the Citron report dated Jan 30 2011 (Pomerantz class action and some sources cite Jan 31 2011, the market-reaction day; John Hempton/Bronte Capital were short earlier but published after Citron). An anonymous whistleblower emailed Deloitte May 23 2010 (non-public). Pre-revelation (before 2011-01-30) issuer periodic reports per data.sec.gov: FY2009 Form 10-K (filed 2010-03-31) + 3 Form 10-Q (2010 Q1 filed 2010-05-14, Q2 filed 2010-08-13, Q3 filed 2010-11-09) = 4; earlier TM Entertainment SPAC 10-K/10-Q (2007-2009) exist under the CIK but predate the operating business and reflect only trust financials. All isXBRL flags = 0.

candidates.company_name: Weatherford International Ltd. (Switzerland); n/k/a Weatherford International plc
candidates.revelation_source: 2011-03-01 announcement that Weatherford would restate 2007-2010 results and had a material weakness in income-tax ICFR; order para.55: 'On March 1, 2011, Weatherford filed a Form 8-K' (EDGAR also shows an NT 10-K filed the same day); stock fell ~11% ($2.38) to $21.14 on 2011-03-02 (per SEC order 33-10221 para.3, 55; 8-K https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1603923/000160392316000247/a8-ksecinvestigationsettle.htm)
candidates.scheme_summary: Made unsupported manual post-closing income-tax 'plug' adjustments (intercompany 'dividend exclusion' entries booked to Luxembourg entities in FY2007-08 and a Bermuda entity in FY2009-10, all within Weatherford's corporate 'Eliminations region') that lowered the year-end tax provision by $100M-$154M/year, understating tax expense/effective tax rate and creating a phantom ~$461M income-tax receivable to align results with previously-touted ETR guidance (per SEC order 33-10221/AAER-3806 para.24, 30, 39, 43-44).
candidates.notes: This is the 2016 INCOME-TAX ACCOUNTING FRAUD AAER (AAER-3806, order 33-10221, 2016-09-27, $140M penalty) - NOT Weatherford's separate FCPA matter. A related 2016-10-18 order (34-79109, AAER-3807) sanctioned auditor Ernst & Young (Rule 102(e)); excluded here as auditor-discipline. Order covers 2007-2012 (>$900M cumulative earnings inflation); core Hudgins/Kitay tax 'plug' fraud was 2007-2010. CIK: the entity that filed the restatements and was the issuer at revelation was Weatherford International Ltd. (Switzerland), CIK 0001453090 (used here); it later became Weatherford International plc, CIK 0001603923 (2014+); a predecessor Bermuda entity filed pre-Feb-2009. This multi-CIK history is why pre_revelation_quarters_available is null (pre-revelation periodic filings are split across CIKs). XBRL: large accelerated filer, XBRL mandatory for periods ending after 2009-06-15, so pre-revelation (through 2010) 10-K/10-Qs carry XBRL. scheme_type mapped to liability_understatement (understated tax expense/liability) + asset_overstatement (phantom tax receivable); primary characterization is income-tax accounting fraud. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->34 = Bermuda predecessor CIK 0001170565 (27: 2002~2009-02, incl. FY2007 10-K 2008-02-21 and FY2008 10-K 2009-02-24) + Swiss CIK 0001453090 (7: 2009-05~2010-11). Bermuda-CIK question in the dossier resolved. scheme_summary and revelation_source corrected per order para.30/39/43-44 (Luxembourg FY2007-08, Bermuda FY2009-10, entity table para.44) and para.55 (vehicle = Form 8-K; EDGAR shows NT 10-K + 8-K both filed 2011-03-01).

candidates.revelation_source: Keyuan 8-K / PRNewswire press release announcing a delayed 2010 Form 10-K, an audit-committee independent investigation into 'unexplained issues of certain cash transactions and recorded sales,' and a requested NASDAQ trading halt (Apr 1 2011)
candidates.scheme_summary: In its first year as a US public company, systematically failed to disclose numerous material related-party transactions (product sales, raw-material purchases, loan guarantees, short-term financing with its CEO/controlling shareholders and their affiliates) and ran a secret off-balance-sheet cash account, misstating reported cash, receivables, CIP and expenses (per SEC LR-22627 / AAER-3447)
candidates.notes: NASDAQ; Nevada shell (Silver Pearl Enterprises) reverse-merged into Keyuan April 2010. Company AAER is AAER-3447 (LR-22627, 2013-02-28); a separate AAER-3472 (2013-07-08) is a Rule 102(e) order against former CFO Aichun Li and is NOT used here. First public revelation = the April 1 2011 8-K/press release; the underlying issues were raised privately by auditor KPMG to the audit committee via a March 28 2011 memo (non-public). pre_revelation_quarters_available = 3 (Form 10-Q for Q1, Q2, Q3 2010) is LOW confidence: Q2 2010 (filed ~2010-08-16) and Q3 2010 (filed ~2010-11-15) are confirmed by the SEC's restatement of 'the second and third quarters of 2010'; a Q1 2010 10-Q (due ~May 2010) is inferred from the SEC's 'periodic reports filed between May 2010 and January 2011' language but not independently confirmed (could be 2). The FY2010 Form 10-K was delayed and not filed until ~Oct 2011 (post-revelation). Shell-era Silver Pearl 10-QSB/10-KSB filings under this CIK are excluded as pre-operating. isXBRL flags on the 2010 pre-revelation filings = 0 (XBRL flags appear only on 2016 filings), so xbrl_available = false. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters 3->2: the 2010-04-19 10-Q (accession 0001121781-10-000181, primary doc sp10q33110.htm) was filed by the pre-merger shell -- EDGAR formerNames shows 'Silver Pearl Enterprises, Inc.' until 2010-05-10; complaint para.14 dates the reverse merger only to 'April 2010' (no day) and para.20 defines the misstated-filings window as May 2010-January 2011. Operating-era pre-revelation periodic reports = Q2+Q3 2010 10-Qs.

candidates.revelation_source: Alfred Little (Jon Carnes) short-seller article alleging undisclosed transfers of Shanxi Coal ownership by Chairman Ming Zhao (Apr 8 2011); Puda confirmed via 8-K Apr 11 2011 and NYSE halted trading
candidates.scheme_summary: Chairman secretly transferred Puda's 90% stake in its sole operating subsidiary (Shanxi Coal) to himself in Sept 2009 and later sold/pledged it to CITIC Trust, while SEC filings and two 2010 offerings still told US investors Puda owned 90%; a forged CITIC letter was given to SEC staff and the audit committee (per SEC LR-22264 / AAER-3372)
candidates.notes: NYSE Amex (formerly AMEX); Nevada shell (Purezza Group) became Puda Coal 2005. Charges (LR-22264 / AAER-3372, 2012-02-22) were against Chairman Ming Zhao and ex-CEO Liping Zhu; final default judgments >$250M entered 2015 (LR-23311). First revelation was the Alfred Little report dated April 8 2011, expressly referenced in Puda's own 8-K. pre_revelation_quarters_available counts modern Form 10-K (FY2008 filed 2009-05-14, FY2009 filed 2010-03-31, FY2010 filed 2011-03-16 = 3) + Form 10-Q (2008 Q1-Q3, 2009 Q1-Q3, 2010 Q1-Q3 = 9) = 12, all filed before 2011-04-08; count is MEDIUM confidence because the data.sec.gov JSON is long and additional earlier 10-KSB (approx FY2005-FY2007) and 10-QSB (2006-2007) filings under this CIK were not individually counted (would add roughly 8-9 more if SB-variants are included). xbrl_available marked false with LOW confidence: the isXBRL array segment was truncated in the fetched JSON so flags were not directly read; comparable China-RTO issuers here (RINO, CCME) show no XBRL, but Puda may have been an accelerated filer potentially subject to XBRL for periods ending after 2010-06-15 (Q2/Q3 2010, FY2010) — a human should verify. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters 12->16: the 2007 filings (10-Q 2007-05-21/08-14/11-14 + FY2007 10-K 2008-04-10) are regular 10-K/10-Q forms, not SB variants as previously assumed. xbrl_available=false now CONFIRMED from EDGAR isXBRL flags (all 0, incl. FY2010 10-K and 2010 Q2/Q3 10-Qs) -- the truncation caveat is resolved.

candidates.revelation_source: Monsanto's disclosure around its fiscal Q3 2011 earnings (~2011-06-29) that the SEC had issued a subpoena / opened a formal investigation into its 'customer incentive' (rebate) programs for glyphosate (Roundup); reported by HuffPost 2011-06-30 and confirmed by Monsanto ('first disclosed the investigation in 2011')
candidates.scheme_summary: Booked revenue from Roundup sales incentivized by rebate programs but improperly deferred recognizing the related rebate costs (incl. via side agreements) and recorded certain rebates as SG&A rather than reductions of revenue, materially overstating FY2009-2011 earnings and Roundup gross profit (per SEC AAER-3741 / Release 33-10037).
candidates.notes: NYSE: MON; fiscal year ends Aug 31; acquired by Bayer in 2018. AAER-3741 = settled administrative order (Release 33-10037, File 3-17107), no restatement required by the settlement because Monsanto had already restated FY2009-Q3 FY2011 in November 2011 after disclosing the SEC probe. first_revelation_date 2011-06-29 is derived from secondary sources (HuffPost 2011-06-30 'announced Wednesday'; Monsanto states it 'first disclosed the investigation in 2011'); a primary EDGAR filing pinning the exact day was not confirmed in this environment, hence medium confidence - a human should verify against the Q3 FY2011 10-Q/8-K. Pre-revelation 10-K/10-Q = 41 (all in older submissions chunk, 2000-2011); 6 of the 41 have XBRL (filings from Q2 FY2010 filed 2010-01-08 and the FY2010 10-K filed 2010-10-27 onward; pre-2010 filings isXBRL=0), so xbrl_available=true (partial). scheme_type medium: conduct is rebate cost-deferral / matching and SG&A-vs-contra-revenue misclassification, mapped to revenue_recognition + liability_understatement. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters 41->43 (10-K405 FY2000 + 10-KT transition report included as annual reports). Order confirms FY2009-2011 framing (para.1-2, fn.2 FY ends Aug 31); earliest described conduct: Canada price-protection program April 2009 (para.37), US sales-force communications late May 2009 (para.15). Order does NOT date the 2011-06-29 investigation disclosure (only the 2011-11-14 restatement, para.3) -- recorded date remains secondary-sourced.

candidates.company_name: Miller Energy Resources, Inc. (f/k/a Miller Petroleum, Inc.)
candidates.revelation_source: 2011-07-28 TheStreetSweeper report (published on Seeking Alpha), 'Miller Energy: This Hot Alaska Stock May Be About to Melt', alleging the Alaska asset valuation was grossly inflated; shares fell >50% over following days (https://archive.knoxnews.com/business/miller-energy-takes-a-beating-in-market-after-blog-post-ep-403495004-357635311.html)
candidates.scheme_summary: Recorded Alaska oil-and-gas assets (acquired for ~$2.25M cash plus ~$2M assumed liabilities) at an overstated ~$480M using a reserve report that was not fair value, double-counting $110M of fixed assets and booking a $277M bargain-purchase gain, overstating assets and net income (per SEC order 33-10002/AAER-3731).
candidates.notes: Fiscal year ends April 30. Alaska assets acquired Dec 2009; fraudulent valuation first appeared in Form 10-Q for fiscal Q3 FY2010 (quarter ended Jan 2010) and FY2010 10-K, and persisted in 10-Qs/10-Ks through fiscal 2015 (misstatement carried until ~2015 restatement/bankruptcy). Multiple AAERs in matter (File 3-16729): original OIP 33-9881/AAER-3673 (2015-08-06); company settlement 33-10002/AAER-3731 (2016-01-12, $5M penalty); June 2016 individual orders AAER-3778 (Boyd), AAER-3779 (Hall), AAER-3780 (Vogt); auditor KPMG order AAER (2022). document_forgery reflects Alaska CEO David Hall altering a report to appear as a third-party valuation. pre_revelation_quarters_available null: public filer since 1996 (form[] array not reliably extractable from truncated JSON). XBRL: filer categorized 'Accelerated filer' (Phase-3 XBRL mandate = periods ending after 2011-06-15); all pre-revelation periodic filings ended before that (FY2011 ended 2011-04-30; FY2011 10-K filed 2011-07-29, one day AFTER revelation), so pre-revelation filings likely lack XBRL - flagged medium. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->16 (EDGAR exact). All scheme figures confirmed from order 33-10002 (para.1-3, 10, 28, 48, 55-57). period end 2015-03 is an inference from para.57 ('Forms 10-Q for... all three quarters of fiscal years 2011 through 2015', FY ends Apr 30 -> Q3 FY2015 10-Q filed ~Mar 2015), not a printed date.

candidates.revelation_source: 2011-09-29 8-K / press release establishing a Special Committee to investigate the accounting of AgFeed's Chinese operations; stock fell ~38% over two sessions to $0.40 on 2011-10-03 (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1331427/000114420411055607/v236105_ex99-1.htm)
candidates.scheme_summary: Reported ~$239M of fictitious China revenue (2008-mid 2011) via fake feed invoices, sales of nonexistent hogs and inflated hog weights, maintaining two sets of books, plus inflated fixed assets; U.S. management then schemed to delay disclosure (per SEC complaint/press release 2014-47 and related admin order 33-9557/AAER-3542).
candidates.notes: AAER caveat: The SEC's action against AgFeed the ISSUER (and the four China defendants, Gothner, Pazdro) was a DISTRICT-COURT COMPLAINT filed 2014-03-11 (Case 3:14-cv-00663, M.D. Tenn.; press release 2014-47) - a litigation release, and I could NOT confirm a distinct company-specific AAER number for that complaint. The AAER cited here (AAER-3542, order 33-9557, 2014-03-11) is the SAME-MATTER settled administrative order against AgFeed's post-merger CFO Clayton T. Marshall; it establishes the matter has AAER coverage. A human verifier should confirm whether the AgFeed company litigation release carries its own AAER. AgFeed: Nevada corp, HQ Hendersonville TN, China RTO; NASDAQ 'FEED' (delisted 2012-03-24), Chapter 11 in 2013; settled for $18M (2014). pre_revelation_quarters_available = 15 derived from the complaint's Appendix A enumerating false periodic filings: 4 10-Ks (3/13/2008, 3/16/2009, 3/8/2010, 3/16/2011) + 11 10-Qs (5/15/2008 through 8/9/2011), all filed before the 2011-09-29 revelation; any pre-2008 filings would have been smaller-company 10-KSB/10-QSB (not counted). XBRL: the 2011-08-09 Q2-2011 10-Q (period ended 2011-06-30) falls under the Phase-3 XBRL mandate and predates revelation, so at least that filing carries XBRL; earlier ones likely do not. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] xbrl true->false: EDGAR isXBRL=0 on every pre-revelation 10-K/10-Q including the 2011-08-09 Q2-2011 10-Q (the Phase-3 assumption did not hold). AAER-3542 confirmed printed on the Marshall order 33-9557 header; companion Stadler order 33-9556 carries NO AAER line; neither the company complaint nor press release 2014-47 prints an AAER number.

candidates.revelation_source: 8-K / press release dated 2011-11-01 announcing Audit Committee investigation into accounting for crop payments to walnut growers; shares fell ~20% on 2011-11-02 (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1320947/000119312511290765/d251266d8k.htm)
candidates.scheme_summary: Delayed/underreported 'continuity' and 'momentum' payments to walnut growers to understate walnut costs (COGS) and grower payables, inflating FY2010-FY2011 earnings to beat analyst estimates (per SEC LR-22902/AAER-3527 and admin order 33-9508/AAER-3526).
candidates.notes: Two AAERs same matter, both 2014-01-09: AAER-3527 = SEC v. Diamond Foods & CFO Steven Neil (district-court complaint, LR-22902); AAER-3526 = settled admin order vs former CEO Mendes (33-9508). Company action reported under AAER-3527. Scheme is cost/earnings-timing manipulation; enum lacks an exact 'expense-understatement' category, so mapped to liability_understatement (understated grower payables) + reserves_smoothing (earnings management to hit targets). pre_revelation_quarters_available not reliably countable from truncated submissions JSON (form[] array not captured); reasoned estimate ~25 (7 10-Ks FY2005-FY2011 filed through 2011-09-15 + ~18 10-Qs). XBRL: FY2011 10-K filed 2011-09-15 (period ended 2011-07-31) falls under the Phase-3 XBRL mandate (periods ending after 2011-06-15) and predates revelation, so at least the final pre-revelation 10-K has XBRL; earlier quarters likely do not. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->25 (EDGAR exact; prior estimate ~25 matched). xbrl true->false: isXBRL=0 on all pre-revelation filings incl. FY2011 10-K filed 2011-09-15. AAER split confirmed from printed headers: LR-22902 page prints AAER-3527 (company+Neil complaints), 33-9508 header prints AAER-3526 (Mendes).

candidates.revelation_source: Orthofix press release issued the evening of 2013-07-29 and Form 8-K (Item 8.01) filed 2013-07-30 announcing the Audit Committee's independent review of prior-period revenue recognition (documented in Orthofix CORRESP and 8-K on EDGAR)
candidates.scheme_summary: Materially overstated distributor revenue and operating income by improperly recognizing revenue on contingent/return-eligible distributor sales (incl. shipping implants unusable without delayed instruments) and treating price discounts as expense rather than reductions of revenue (per SEC AAER-3845 / Release 33-10281).
candidates.notes: NASDAQ: OFIX; incorporated in Curacao, HQ Texas, files as US domestic filer (10-K/10-Q). AAER-3845 is the accounting order (Release 33-10281); a SIMULTANEOUS separate FCPA order (AAER-3851 / Release 34-79828) exists but concerns FCPA/Brazil bribery and is excluded. Restatement covered FY2010-Q1 2013 and also corrected inventory reserves and royalties (scheme_type kept to primary revenue_recognition). Pre-revelation 10-K/10-Q = 43: 5 in recent submissions (2012-2013) + 38 in older chunk (10 x 10-K, 28 x 10-Q, 2003-03-31 to 2012-03-25); pre-2003 annual reports were filed on Form 20-F (earlier FPI era) and are not counted. XBRL present from ~FY2011 filings (filed 2012) onward; earlier filings isXBRL=0, so xbrl_available=true (partial). [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters 43->42 (EDGAR exact: 11 10-K + 31 10-Q; prior count was 1 high). Order's printed period: 'From at least 2011 to mid-2013' (Summary); restatement also covered FY2010 annual (para.92). Order does not describe the 2013-07-29 announcement -- recorded date remains EDGAR/secondary-sourced (8-K 2013-07-30 confirmed in EDGAR window).

candidates.revelation_source: Logitech announcement (2014-05-21) that its Audit Committee, with independent advisors, had begun an independent investigation of certain accounting matters relating to previously issued financial statements (disclosed in FY2014 Form 10-K, R9.htm)
candidates.scheme_summary: Fraudulently delayed a ~$30.7M lower-of-cost-or-market write-down of discontinued 'Revue' set-top-box inventory to inflate FY2011 operating income ~27% and meet guidance; also understated FY12-13 warranty liabilities, failed to amortize acquired intangibles, and committed FY09 Americas revenue-recognition/controls violations (per SEC AAER-3765 / Release 34-77644).
candidates.notes: NASDAQ: LOGI (also SIX: LOGN). Swiss company reporting as a US domestic filer (10-K/10-Q) since FY2010; before mid-2009 it filed Form 20-F (older chunk, 1997-2009), which is excluded from the 10-K/10-Q count. Pre-revelation 10-K/10-Q = 19 (all in recent, back to FY2010 Q1 filed 2009-08-10). first_revelation set to 2014-05-21 (investigation disclosure); note an EARLIER candidate 2013-08-07 FY2013 10-K/A that disclosed a Revue-inventory-valuation material weakness, and the formal Item 4.02 non-reliance came 2014-09-02/03. XBRL mixed across pre-rev filings (several FY2010 and early FY2011/FY2012 filings isXBRL=0; the FY2011 10-K filed 2011-05-27 and most filings from late-2010 onward isXBRL=1), so xbrl_available=true (partial). Individuals Bardman/Wolf charged by district-court complaint (LR-23523); company/Doktorczyk/Bolles settled by order (AAER-3765). [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters 19->24: LOGI filed domestic 10-K/10-Q from FY2008 (10-K 2008-05-30, rpt 2008-03-31), not FY2010 as assumed. manipulation_period_start 2009-04->2008-04: order 34-77644 covers 'a five-year period' = FY09-FY13 with FY = Apr 1-Mar 31 (fn.2); AMR revenue conduct ran 'from late FY08 through FY09' (para.55, i.e. from ~Jan-Mar 2008 -- even earlier than the corrected month); restatement covered FY09-FY13 (para.54). The prior 2009-04 start mapped 'FY09' to calendar 2009 -- a fiscal-calendar slip.

candidates.revelation_source: June 6, 2014 Form 8-K (Item 4.02): Audit Committee concluded FY2011 financials should no longer be relied upon and would be restated, with likely further adjustments to 2012-2013. https://www.streetinsider.com/Corporate+News/Hertz+Global+(HTZ)+Discloses+Results+of+Internal+Audit;+Will+Restate+Certain+Financials/10014062.html
candidates.scheme_summary: Materially overstated pre-tax income across multiple business units through improper accounting for allowances/reserves and expense timing, and extended U.S. rental-fleet holding periods to lower depreciation expense without adequate disclosure, amid pressure to meet internal budgets and estimates (per SEC AAER-4012 / press release 33-10601-s).
candidates.notes: CIK ambiguity for a human to resolve: the AAER respondent 'Hertz Global Holdings, Inc.' at settlement (Dec 2018) is the post-2016-reorg entity CIK 0001657853 (ticker HTZ), reported here. BUT the misstated FY2011-FY2013 10-Ks were filed by the PREDECESSOR issuer, also named Hertz Global Holdings, Inc., CIK 0001364479 (which after the 2016 spin-off was renamed Herc Holdings Inc.). Use CIK 0001364479 to retrieve the pre-revelation (pre-2014-06-06) 10-K/10-Q filings. The Hertz Corporation subsidiary is CIK 0000047129 and was a co-respondent. SEC press release states misstatements 'from February 2012 through March 2014'; the July 2015 restatement also revised FY2011 and identified ~$235M in overstated pre-tax income. Unverified pre-revelation 10-K/10-Q estimate (filer 1364479, before 2014-06-06): ~7 10-Ks (FY2006-FY2012) + ~21 10-Qs = ~28; the FY2013 10-K and Q1-2014 10-Q were delayed by the restatement and not filed before revelation. Could not verify count from data.sec.gov submissions JSON (array truncation via this interface). [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->29 (predecessor CIK 1364479 included). Order caption has TWO respondents: Hertz Global Holdings, Inc. AND The Hertz Corporation. Order para.33 describes an EARLIER public signal: 2014-05-13 announcement of inability to file Q1-2014 10-Q with possible FY2011 restatement -- a first_revelation alternative for the Monday principle decision (para.34 = the recorded June 6 event).

candidates.company_name: Penn West Petroleum Ltd. (now Obsidian Energy Ltd.)
candidates.revelation_source: Penn West news release 'Announces Management-Initiated Internal Review of Accounting Practices and Decision to Restate Certain Historical Financial Statements' (2014-07-29), stating 2012/2013/Q1-2014 (and prior-four-year) financials should not be relied upon; company notified SEC and Alberta Securities Commission (also furnished on Form 6-K)
candidates.scheme_summary: Fraudulently reclassified hundreds of millions of dollars of operating expenses as capital expenditures (and as royalty expense) via large, round-number, unsupported 'reclass to capital' journal entries to hit internal opex/boe targets and understate operating costs (per SEC AAER-4133 / complaint via LR-24809).
candidates.notes: Foreign private issuer (Canadian). At the time listed NYSE: PWE / TSX: PWT; renamed Obsidian Energy Ltd. (NYSE/TSX: OBE) in June 2017. Files annual reports on Form 40-F (IFRS) and furnishes interim quarterly financials within Form 6-K (does NOT file 10-K/10-Q). pre_revelation_quarters_available set to null because quarterly-financial 6-Ks cannot be reliably isolated from other 6-Ks by form type: 8 annual reports on Form 40-F (2007-2014, incl. one 40-F/A) and 276 total 6-K submissions were filed before 2014-07-29. xbrl_available=false: every pre-revelation filing has isXBRL=0 (40-F filers did not submit XBRL in this period). AAER-4133 (2020-04-28) accompanied the settlement with former CFO Takeyasu and VP Curran; the company itself was charged 2017-06-28 (LR-24195) and settled for $8.5M by court judgment 2017-11-20 (LR-23993). Canonical AAER-4133 URL not directly reachable in this environment; the LR-24809 page carries the AAER number.

candidates.revelation_source: Bankrate Form 8-K (Item 4.02 non-reliance for FY2011-2013 and Item 5.02 CFO Edward DiMaria resignation) plus press release announcing a formal SEC investigation of the company's 2012 financial reporting, 2014-09-15
candidates.scheme_summary: Fabricated ~$0.8M of revenue accruals and improperly reduced/withheld expense accruals (incl. reversing a marketing 'cushion'/cookie-jar accrual and not booking known SOX fees) to inflate Q2 2012 adjusted EBITDA/EPS and meet analyst estimates (per SEC AAER-3683 / Release 33-9901).
candidates.notes: NYSE: RATE. Company settled via administrative cease-and-desist order = AAER-3683 (Release 33-9901, Admin Proc. 3-16786). Executives DiMaria/Gamsey were charged by district-court complaint (LR-23335, 2015-09-08) and Lerner by settled order; those individual actions are not separate company AAERs. Core AAER conduct is Q2 2012 (quarter ended 6/30/2012); a marketing 'cushion' accrual had been built up for over a year prior (from ~2011), and the 9/14/2014 non-reliance covered FY2011, 2012 and 2013 - so a human may prefer a wider manipulation window. Pre-revelation 10-K/10-Q = 13 (3 x 10-K, 10 x 10-Q); Bankrate IPO'd June 2011 and all 13 filings have XBRL (isXBRL=1). filings.files empty (complete history in recent).

candidates.revelation_source: Iconix Q2-2015 report disclosing an SEC 'comment letter process'/ongoing review of the 2014 Form 10-K questioning GAAP accounting for the international joint-venture gains (core of the fraud); shares fell on the news - https://wwd.com/fashion-news/ready-to-wear/iconix-sec-review-joint-venture-neil-cole-10199297/
candidates.scheme_summary: Fabricated licensing revenue in 2014 to beat consensus and failed to timely recognize $239M+ of brand impairment while entering transactions to prop up distressed licensees, overstating net income (per SEC Litigation Release LR-24682 / press release 2019-251)
candidates.notes: SEC pursued Iconix (the issuer) via a settled path plus federal-court litigation against CEO Cole; the issuer/CFO Clamen matter was resolved by a settled administrative order dated 2019-12-05 (accountant suspension for Clamen) that almost certainly carries an AAER number, but I could not confirm the specific AAER number from reachable sources - do not assume. Iconix agreed to a $5.5M penalty. First-revelation date is judgment-dependent: the 2015-08-10 disclosure of the SEC comment-letter review of the JV accounting is the earliest clearly accounting-specific market signal, but CEO/CFO/COO departures and class actions predate it; see revelation_candidates. pre_revelation_quarters_available left null (countable via data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK0000857737.json but not reliably parseable within tool constraints). [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->73 (EDGAR exact). The 2019-12-05 admin order is 33-10730, respondent Warren Clamen (CPA, ex-CFO) ONLY, and its header prints ACCOUNTING AND AUDITING ENFORCEMENT Release No. 4105 -- the matter thus HAS an AAER, but executives-only (same axis as T27 WAGE). Whether an exec-only AAER counts as the issuer's AAER = Monday inclusion decision; aaer_no left null pending that rule. Company was sued separately: SEC v. Iconix Brand Group, 19-CV-11150 (comp-pr2019-251-iconix), $5.5M penalty per PR 2019-251/LR-24682.

candidates.revelation_source: September 11, 2015 8-K/press release: Marvell's Audit Committee announced an independent investigation of revenue-recognition and internal-control matters and delayed its Q2 fiscal-2016 10-Q, triggering NASDAQ non-compliance. https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/marvell-technology-group-ltd-announces-completion-of-audit-committee-investigation-300228408.html
candidates.scheme_summary: Orchestrated an undisclosed 'pull-in' revenue-management scheme, accelerating sales originally scheduled for future quarters into the current quarter to close the gap to publicly issued revenue guidance and mask a decline in customer demand, without disclosing the scheme to the board or its auditor (per SEC AAER-4076 / press release 2019-175).
candidates.notes: US-listed on NASDAQ (MRVL); Bermuda-incorporated. Order release 33-10684 / 34-86971, AAER-4076, admin file 3-19454; $5.5M penalty. Charged as misleading-disclosure / issuer reporting violations (Securities Act 17(a)(2); Exchange Act 13(a)) for failing to disclose the revenue-management scheme - Marvell did NOT restate. Manipulation period is LOW confidence due to fiscal-calendar ambiguity: Marvell's fiscal year ends late January, and sources describe pull-ins of ~$24M and ~$64M in 'Q4 2015 and Q1 2016' (i.e., roughly fiscal Q4 2015 ~Nov 2014-Jan 2015 and fiscal Q1 2016 ~Feb-May 2015); the audit-committee probe centered on Q2 fiscal 2016 (ended Aug 1, 2015). Human should confirm exact quarters from the order text. Legacy entity CIK 0001058057 (Marvell Technology Group Ltd); a 2021 reorganization created Marvell Technology Inc CIK 0001835632. Unverified pre-revelation 10-K/10-Q estimate (before 2015-09-11): ~15 10-Ks + ~44 10-Qs (public since 2000 IPO). Could not verify from submissions JSON (truncation). [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->60 (EDGAR exact). Order-anchored quarters: charged = Q4 FY2015 ended 2015-01-31, $24M pull-ins (para.12) + Q1 FY2016 ended 2015-05-02, $64M (para.18); pull-ins were USED in three quarters (para.10) incl. Q2 FY2016 ended 2015-08-01, record $77M (para.21). '$165M' is arithmetic (24+64+77), not printed in the order. Order's own 'Relevant Period' = 'approximately January 2015 through July 2015' (para.2). Which window defines manipulation_period = Monday principle decision; recorded values unchanged.

candidates.company_name: Valeant Pharmaceuticals International, Inc. (n/k/a Bausch Health Companies Inc.)
candidates.revelation_source: October 19, 2015 Southern Investigative Reporting Foundation expose ('The King's Gambit') first detailing undisclosed Valeant-Philidor ties; followed by the Oct 21, 2015 Citron Research report alleging channel-stuffing/'phantom pharmacies,' after which the stock was halted down ~28%. https://www.cnbc.com/2015/10/21/valeant-halted-in-heavy-trading-down-28-after-citron-research-report.html
candidates.scheme_summary: Improperly recognized revenue from sales to Philidor, an undisclosed captive mail-order pharmacy Valeant helped establish/fund/subsidize, and touted the resulting non-GAAP same-store organic growth for five consecutive quarters, while failing to disclose the Philidor relationship and misallocating revenue from a 500% price increase on an acquired drug across 100+ unrelated products (per SEC AAER-4153 / press release 2020-169).
candidates.notes: US-listed on NYSE (VRX) and TSX at the time; Canadian-domiciled but a U.S. domestic filer (10-K/10-Q), CIK 0000885590 (same CIK now used by Bausch Health; predecessor lineage includes Biovail/ICN). Settled 2020-07-31 under Securities Act Sec. 17(a)(2)/(3) and Reg G; company also consented to reporting, books-and-records, and internal-controls findings; total ~$45M program including related individual orders (Pearson AAER, Schiller, Carro). first_revelation_date is medium confidence: the R&O lawsuit (~Oct 5, 2015) surfaced Philidor first, but broad market recognition that Valeant's accounting/disclosure was wrong crystallized with the Oct 19 SIRF and Oct 21 Citron reports. Manipulation period 'beginning in 2014' per order (Philidor five consecutive quarters plus the April 2015 price-increase misallocation); exact end quarter not precisely stated. Unverified pre-revelation 10-K/10-Q estimate (domestic forms, before 2015-10-19): ~5 10-Ks (FY2010-FY2014) + ~14 10-Qs; earlier Biovail-era filings were foreign forms (40-F/6-K). Could not verify from submissions JSON (truncation). [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->23 (domestic forms only, EDGAR exact). manipulation_period_start 2014-01->2014-07: order 33-10809 para.15/15a anchors the misleading reporting to quarters ended 2014-09-30 through 2015-09-30 (Q3 2014 begins July 2014); no order language supports January 2014. Alternative under a 'scheme conduct' principle: Philidor was established/funded from 2013 (para.2, 8) and the last misleading filing act was the FY2015 10-K filed 2016-04-29 (para.25) -- Monday principle decision if a different convention is preferred.

candidates.revelation_source: Q3 2015 Form 10-Q filed 2015-11-16 disclosing an ICFR material weakness and correcting revenue recognition on three distributor contracts (-$1.8M Q1 2015, -$1.0M Q2 2015, +$0.8M Q3 2015, -$1.1M 2014), followed by Form 8-K Item 4.02 filed 2015-11-20 (per SEC complaint 17-cv-03230 para.29 and the 8-K https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1360886/000110465915080467/a15-23865_18k.htm); shares fell ~21.5% on 2015-11-17 (secondary sources)
candidates.scheme_summary: Artificially inflated reported revenue by recognizing revenue at inflated prices, backdating documents to shift revenue into earlier periods, prematurely recognizing revenue on consignment deliveries, using pricing data known to be false, and booking a fictitious transaction (per SEC AAER-3905 / LR-23978).
candidates.notes: This was a CIVIL injunctive action (SEC v. Osiris Therapeutics, et al., D. Md. 17-cv-03230), not an administrative order, but it carries AAER-3905 (confirmed via NYU SEED). Osiris settled for a $1.5M penalty (Nov 2017); litigation continued against four former officers (CEO Debrabandere, CFOs Jacoby and Law, CBO Montgomery). aaer_date 2017-11-02 = SEC press release date; litigation release LR-23978 dated Nov 3, 2017; complaint filed ~Nov 1-2, 2017. Fraud period per complaint: all four quarters of 2014 and first three quarters of 2015. Osiris was acquired by Smith & Nephew in 2019 and deregistered. Unverified pre-revelation 10-K/10-Q estimate (before 2015-11-16): ~9 10-Ks (FY2006-FY2014) + ~27-28 10-Qs; Q3-2015 10-Q was delayed. Could not verify count from submissions JSON (truncation). [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->37 (EDGAR exact). aaer_date 2017-11-02->2017-11-03: LR-23978 header prints 'Accounting and Auditing Enforcement Release No. 3905 / November 3, 2017' (the complaint was filed 2017-11-02 -- kept as complaint-date context). revelation_source corrected: no 8-K exists on 2015-11-16 (that day's filing is the Q3 10-Q, accession 0001558370-15-002707); the previously cited URL (0001104659-16-105218) is a LATER 8-K filed 2016-03-15 (Items 3.01/4.02/8.01, complaint para.30). EARLIER public signals exist: 8-K 2015-11-06 (Q3 earnings release disclosing the distributor-contract review) and NT 10-Q 2015-11-10 ('does not expect... material impact') -- which event is 'first revelation' = Monday principle decision.

candidates.revelation_source: Brixmor 8-K / press release dated Feb 8, 2016 announcing resignations of CEO, CFO, CAO and an accounting employee following an Audit Committee review that found income-smoothing of SP NOI; stock fell ~20%. Source: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1581068/000119312516453080/d121667dex991.htm
candidates.scheme_summary: Manipulated the non-GAAP 'same property net operating income' (SP NOI) growth rate by smoothing income up and down between quarters using a 'cookie jar' ledger account (the '2617 account'), including previously-excluded lease-termination income, and lowering prior-period SP NOI, to report steady growth matching guidance (per SEC Order 34-86538 / AAER-4061).
candidates.notes: Settled administrative order against the company (Rel. 34-86538, AAER-4061, File 3-19300, Aug 1, 2019). Parallel civil complaint (SEC v. Carroll et al., LR-24577) and DOJ criminal charges were brought against four executives; later Rule 102(e) orders carry AAER-4233 (Mortimer) etc. scheme_type tagged reserves_smoothing (cookie-jar income smoothing); note the misstatement was of a NON-GAAP measure (SP NOI) rather than restated GAAP financials — SEC charged Reg G Rule 100(b) plus antifraud; a 'disclosure/non-GAAP' element is present. pre_revelation_quarters_available (9) is cadence-derived (IPO Oct 29, 2013; 2 10-Ks FY2013-FY2014 + ~7 10-Qs Q3 2013-Q3 2015); could be 8 if no separate Q3 2013 10-Q was filed. All filings are XBRL-era.

candidates.revelation_source: comScore Form 12b-25 (NT 10-K) filed 2016-02-29 disclosing that its Audit Committee had an unfinished review of 'certain potential accounting matters'; fuller detail in the March 7, 2016 press release / 8-K delaying the FY2015 10-K. Source: https://ir.comscore.com/news-releases/news-release-details/comscore-inc-delays-filing-annual-report-form-10-k
candidates.scheme_summary: Overstated revenue by approximately $50 million (over $34.5M from improperly valued non-monetary data-exchange transactions plus ~$12M from undisclosed linked/side-agreement monetary contracts) to beat consensus revenue targets for seven straight quarters, plus false statements about key performance metrics (per SEC Order 33-10692/AAER-4091 para.1-3).
candidates.notes: Settled administrative cease-and-desist order (Rel. 33-10692 / 34-87069, AAER-4091, File 3-19499); companion order against ex-CEO Serge Matta is 33-10693/AAER-4092. SEED lists First Document Date 24-Sep-2019 (one SEED highlight text says 'September 17, 2019' — likely order-drafting date; press release and Fair Fund docs use Sept 24, 2019). first_revelation: Feb 29, 2016 12b-25 is the first PUBLIC surfacing (audit committee received the tip internally Feb 19, 2016); some observers date the revelation to the March 7, 2016 press release. pre_revelation_quarters_available (34) is cadence-derived (IPO June 2007; 8 10-Ks FY2007-FY2014 + 26 10-Qs Q2 2007-Q3 2015) from public history, not a raw EDGAR count (submissions JSON too large to fully parse); treat as approximate. XBRL present for the 2011-2015 pre-revelation filings (earliest 2007-2010 filings predate XBRL mandate). [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] scheme_summary $43-50M->~$50M: order uses 'approximately $50 million' throughout (para.1, 5); $43M appears nowhere; components $34.5M NMT (para.2) + ~$12M linked contracts (para.3). aaer_date 2019-09-24 confirmed printed on order header (SEED's 'September 17' note resolved). NMT effects reach back to Q4 2013 (first NMT effective 2013-12-20, para.12) -- period principle nuance for Monday.

candidates.revelation_source: Tangoe 8-K Item 4.02 (event dated March 4, 2016; filed/accepted March 7, 2016) announcing non-reliance on FY2013, FY2014 and the first three quarters of FY2015 financials and a restatement for revenue-recognition errors. Source: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1182325/000110465916102988/a16-5958_18k.htm
candidates.scheme_summary: Overstated revenue by ~$40M of $566M reported by prematurely recognizing revenue for unperformed work, booking customer prepayments and uncollectible/contingent-fee revenue, recording a business partner's loan as revenue, and violating bad-debt reserve rules; an executive falsified business records provided to auditors (per SEC complaint, LR-24255).
candidates.notes: SEC action was a civil injunctive complaint / Litigation Release LR-24255 (Sept 4, 2018, D. Conn., settled) — NO AAER number: Tangoe does not appear in the SEC AAER index for 2018 (releases around LR-24255 do not include it) and SEED lists no AAER for the action, so aaer_no set null (not fabricated). Company was taken private by Marlin Equity in 2017 (delisted; 15-12B filed June 2017), hence no current ticker in EDGAR (was Nasdaq: TNGO). pre_revelation_quarters_available (17) verified against data.sec.gov filingDate array: 4 10-Ks (FY2011-FY2014) + 13 10-Qs (Q3 2011 through Q3 2015, filed Nov 2015). All XBRL-era. document_forgery tag reflects SEC allegation that SVP Donald Farias falsified business records given to external auditors. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters 17->18: first 10-Q was 2011-09-09 (Q2 2011, post-June-2011 IPO), one earlier than assumed. No-AAER status confirmed: LR-24255 page prints no AAER line. $40M = SEC headline/workpaper figure (complaint para.94) vs $30.5M Nov-2016 12b-25 estimate (para.93) -- both real, different bases.

candidates.revelation_source: August 15, 2016 (after close) Hain announced it would delay its FY2016 results, miss guidance, and was evaluating whether revenue tied to distributor concessions was recognized in the correct period; shares fell ~27% on Aug 16, 2016. https://www.cnbc.com/2016/08/16/hains-plunge-puts-revenue-recognition-in-spotlight-again.html
candidates.scheme_summary: Sales personnel offered the company's two largest distributors undocumented end-of-quarter incentives (return rights for spoiled/expired product, cash payments up to $500K, discounts, extended payment terms) to pull inventory forward and meet internal quarterly sales targets; charged as books-and-records and internal-accounting-controls failures with no restatement required (per SEC AAER-3997 / press release 2018-277).
candidates.notes: UNUSUAL OUTCOME (flagged as instructed): despite an AAER (AAER-3997, order release 34-84781, Dec 11 2018), the SEC charged ONLY books-and-records (13(b)(2)(A)) and internal-accounting-controls (13(b)(2)(B)) violations - NO antifraud charge, NO monetary penalty (credited for self-reporting/cooperation), and Hain ultimately concluded in June 2017 that a formal RESTATEMENT was NOT required (it instead disclosed material weaknesses). So it qualifies as an accounting/financial-reporting AAER but is not a classic fraud/restatement case. Not FCPA, not 102(e) auditor discipline, not broker-dealer - so include=true. Incentive practices ran 'between 2014 and 2016' (fiscal quarters); finance dept became aware ~May 2016. SEC subpoena issued Jan 31, 2017. Fiscal year ends June 30; the FY2016 10-K (the delayed filing) came out June 22, 2017. Unverified pre-revelation 10-K/10-Q estimate (before 2016-08-15): ~22 10-Ks (through FY2015) + ~66 10-Qs (public since ~1993). Could not verify from submissions JSON (truncation). [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->79 (EDGAR exact, 10-K405 era included). Order nuances: period printed as 'From at least 2014 until May 2016' / 'FY 2014-2016' (Summary, para.4; FY2014 began 2013-07-01); incentives described in Q4 FY2016 itself (para.14, ~$1.5M) so end 2016-06 defensible; 'spoils coverage' = reimbursement not return rights (para.5); targets were distributor inventory-purchasing targets (para.5-6). Aug 15 2016 self-report + same-day 8-K confirmed (para.19); the ~27% drop is not in the order.

candidates.revelation_source: Prescience Point Capital Management short-seller report on Celadon Group (CGI) published April 5, 2017, alleging overvalued trucks/off-balance-sheet transactions and fabricated financials (price target $0). Source: https://www.presciencepoint.com/research/research-archives/celadon-group-cgi/
candidates.scheme_summary: Avoided recognizing at least ~$20M in used-truck impairment charges/losses (nearly two-thirds of 2016 pre-tax income) by buying and selling more than a thousand trucks at inflated prices (in cases 2-3x fair value) with a third-party dealer, overstating asset values and thereby pre-tax income, net income and EPS in its FY2016 10-K and subsequent filings (per SEC complaint, LR-24459).
candidates.notes: SEC action against the company was a civil complaint / Litigation Release LR-24459 (April 25, 2019, S.D. Ind.; company admitted violations; $7M disgorgement satisfied by DOJ DPA restitution of $42.2M) — NO AAER number: Celadon does not appear in the SEC AAER index for 2019 and SEED lists no AAER for the company action, so aaer_no null. Follow-on 2023 Rule 102(e) orders against execs Meek/Peavler carry AAER-4398/AAER-4397; separate civil charges vs Meek & Peavler (Dec 5, 2019) and vs Danny Williams. Ticker CGI (NYSE) at time of events (Prescience report titled 'Celadon Group | CGI'); shares later moved to OTC (CGIP) after 2018 delisting. first_revelation: Prescience Point report April 5, 2017 is the earliest clear market-moving public allegation; an analyst (Ian Yoon) reportedly questioned the accounting publicly from ~July 2016 (vague) and the formal non-reliance 8-K came May 1, 2017. manipulation period per complaint 'Between June 2016 and April 2017'; misstated filings = FY2016 10-K (period ended 2016-06-30, filed Sept 13, 2016) through Q2 FY2017 10-Q (period ended 2016-12-31). Related-party 19th Capital JV (with Element Fleet) figured heavily in short-seller allegations and a separately disclosed larger balance-sheet issue; the SEC company complaint centers on round-trip truck transactions with a third-party dealer ('Party A'). pre_revelation_quarters_available set null: Celadon has been an SEC filer since ~1994 (FY ends June 30) with roughly 20+ 10-Ks and ~65+ 10-Qs available pre-revelation; exact count not reliably determined without parsing the full (very large) submissions history. XBRL present for filings from ~2011 onward. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->84 (EDGAR exact). No-AAER confirmed: LR-24459 prints no AAER line. All scheme figures confirmed (complaint para.1-2, 19-25; LR for two-thirds/$7M-disgorgement). $42.2M DPA figure appears in NEITHER the LR nor the complaint -- secondary-sourced. Complaint-internal date inconsistency: para.25 dates Q2 FY2017 10-Q 2017-02-10, para.31 says 2017-02-02 (which para.25 lists as the 8-K date).

candidates.revelation_source: Jul 21, 2017 Q2 earnings disclosure of adverse long-term-care insurance claims and announcement of a comprehensive insurance-reserve review (GE whistleblower timeline; CNBC); https://www.cnbc.com/2018/01/24/general-electric-reveals-sec-investigation-of-insurance-reserve-review.html
candidates.scheme_summary: 2015-2017 disclosure failures in the Power and Insurance segments: failed to disclose that a large share of GE Power profit growth (~1/4 of 2016 and ~1/2 of first three quarters of 2017) came from reductions in prior cost estimates and that ~$2.5B of reported industrial cash collections came from internal receivable sales to GE Capital, and failed to disclose known adverse trends/uncertainties in its long-term-care insurance reserves (which later produced a $9.5B pre-tax charge); no restatement of prior financials alleged (per SEC AAER-4194 / press release 2020-312).
candidates.notes: AAER order: 33-10899 / 34-90620 / AAER-4194, Admin Proc 3-20165. Disclosure/MD&A (Item 303) case plus antifraud, reporting and controls; SEC made NO allegation that prior-period financial statements were misstated (GE did separately restate for adoption of the new revenue-recognition standard, unrelated to a fraud finding). Manipulation window is approximate: order describes insurance nondisclosure 'Q3 2015 through Q1 2017' and power nondisclosure across 2016 and first three quarters of 2017 — start/end set to bracket both; Fair Fund loss period was Oct 16 2015-Jan 16 2018. first_revelation is genuinely multi-pronged over 2017-2018; set to the earliest market-learning event tied to the AAER subject (Jul 21 2017 LTC disclosure/review), with the $6.2B charge (Jan 16 2018) and SEC-investigation disclosure (Jan 24 2018) as later candidates — MEDIUM confidence, human should confirm which milestone to use. pre_revelation_quarters_available set to null per instruction (huge filer; submissions JSON truncates across many additional files); xbrl_available true because GE is a large accelerated filer that has filed 10-K/10-Q with XBRL since ~2009. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->94 (EDGAR exact, all 3 submission chunks parsed). Order confirms the TWO-window structure: insurance 'from the third quarter of 2015 through the first quarter of 2017' (Summary, para.36); Power 'Between May 2016 and October 2017' (para.15) with 29%/45% of segment profit from estimate reductions in 2016/first-3Q-2017 (para.6). $2.5B collections (Summary; para.13 separately cites >$2.7B cumulative monetized receivables), $9.5B charge, $200M penalty, no-restatement all confirmed. Order dates the LTC disclosure only as 'In July 2017' (para.35) -- day precision (07-21) remains secondary-sourced.

candidates.revelation_source: Short-seller reports published Sept 20, 2017 by Aurelius Value ('MiMedx: Flying Too Close To The Sun') and Viceroy Research alleging channel stuffing / improper revenue recognition; MiMedx confirmed it was working with the SEC (subpoena) on Sept 21, 2017 and the stock fell. Source: https://www.aureliusvalue.com/research/mimedx-flying-close-sun/
candidates.scheme_summary: Prematurely recognized revenue and exaggerated revenue growth through channel-stuffing to distributors and undisclosed side arrangements with five distributors (allowing product returns or conditioning distributors' payment on resale to end users), then concealed the arrangements from accountants, auditors and the audit committee (per SEC complaint, LR-24678).
candidates.notes: SEC action against the company was a civil complaint / Litigation Release LR-24678 (Nov 26, 2019, S.D.N.Y.; $1.5M penalty) — NO AAER number: MiMedx does not appear in the SEC AAER index for 2019 and SEED lists no AAER for the company action, so aaer_no null. IMPORTANT first_revelation ambiguity: the earliest PUBLIC allegation of the accounting problem was the Dec 15, 2016 Halunen Law whistleblower lawsuit (press-released, alleging channel stuffing) — if 'earliest public allegation' is preferred, first_revelation_date should be 2016-12-15. I set it to 2017-09-20 because that is the first clearly market-moving public exposé (Aurelius Value + Viceroy short-seller reports; stock dropped; company confirmed SEC subpoena Sept 21, 2017); the audit-committee investigation/delayed 10-K was later (Feb 20, 2018) and restatement June 7, 2018. Human verifier should choose between 2016-12-15 and 2017-09-20. pre_revelation_quarters_available set null: MiMedx became an SEC reporting company via Feb 2008 reverse merger (CIK 1376339, formerly Alynx Co); roughly 9 10-Ks (FY2008-FY2016) + ~29 10-Qs would be available before Sept 20, 2017 (~38 total; ~35 if the earlier Dec 15, 2016 date is used), but exact count not reliably determined from the large submissions history and is date-dependent. XBRL present for filings from ~2011 onward. Later Nasdaq delisting (2018); traded OTC PINK as MDXG. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters null->38 (EDGAR exact; estimate matched). Complaint's own disclosure narrative (para.9): fraud 'uncovered and disclosed to the public in a series of announcements from February 2018 through July 2018' (stock -73%, $14.47->$3.93); neither complaint nor LR mentions the Sept 2017 short-seller reports or the Dec 2016 whistleblower suit -- recorded 2017-09-20 rests on non-SEC contemporaneous sources; alternatives (2016-12-15 / 2017-09-20 / 2018-02) = Monday decision.

candidates.revelation_source: Mar 1-2, 2018 WageWorks announced it would be late filing its FY2017 10-K and was investigating revenue recognition on a fiscal-2016 government contract; stock fell >9%; https://www.sec.gov/files/litigation/admin/2021/33-10925.pdf
candidates.scheme_summary: Former CEO Joseph Jackson and CFO Colm Callan made false/misleading statements to WageWorks's internal accountants and its outside auditor about a public-sector client contract (Client A), causing WageWorks to improperly recognize $3.6M of 2016 revenue that was not realizable or collectible; WageWorks restated Q2, Q3 and FY2016 on March 18, 2019 (per SEC AAER-4202 / press release 2021-23).
candidates.notes: AAER order: 33-10925 / 34-91042 / AAER-4202, Admin Proc 3-20217. The settled AAER charged the two former executives (Jackson, CEO; Callan, CFO), NOT WageWorks itself — the company was 'caused' to violate reporting/books-and-records provisions but was not a named respondent and paid no penalty; include=true because the underlying matter is issuer financial-statement manipulation with a restatement, but a human should confirm whether an executives-only AAER counts as the issuer's AAER for this dataset. Manipulation period: $3.6M improperly recognized across Q2, Q3 and FY2016 (contract signed March 1, 2016; 'Base Year 1' March-Sept 2016). pre_revelation_quarters_available = 22 (5 10-K + 17 10-Q) filed before 2018-03-01, all carrying XBRL; complete count (submissions JSON not truncated). WageWorks (NYSE: WAGE) was acquired by HealthEquity in Aug 2019. A related private class-action order references additional OPM-contract and KP Connector issues, but the SEC AAER is limited to the $3.6M Client A revenue. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] Order para.44 verbatim: 'On March 1 and 2, 2018, WageWorks announced that it would be late in filing its fiscal-year 2017 Form 10-K... WageWorks's stock price declined more than 9% on the news.' -- the recorded '>9%' is the ORDER'S OWN language, not a $9.75 transcription slip (dossier hypothesis reversed); contemporaneous class-action sources put the 2018-03-01 one-day move at -$9.75/-18.6% ($52.45->$42.70). Also para.8: Client A's non-payment position was known 2016-03-29 (slightly before the recorded 2016-04-01 period start); recognition began June 2016 (para.11).

candidates.revelation_source: Feb 21, 2019 Q4-2018 earnings release: $15.4B goodwill/intangible write-down, dividend cut, and disclosure of an SEC subpoena tied to procurement accounting; stock fell ~27% next day; https://www.sec.gov/newsroom/press-releases/2021-174
candidates.scheme_summary: Multi-year procurement 'expense management' scheme (Q4 2015-end 2018): improperly/prematurely recognized unearned supplier discounts and rebates (prebate, clawback and price-phasing transactions) and maintained false/misleading supplier contracts to understate cost of goods sold and inflate adjusted EBITDA; ~59 of 295 corrected transactions, driving a June 2019 restatement of $208M in improper cost savings (per SEC AAER-4248 / press release 2021-174).
candidates.notes: AAER order: 33-10977 / 34-92874 / AAER-4248, Admin Proc 3-20523 (settled with KHC and former COO Pelleissone; former CPO Hofmann charged separately in SDNY). scheme_type: the misconduct understated COGS/expenses (not a classic capitalization) and obscured repayment obligations (clawbacks) — tagged 'other' (expense-management/COGS understatement) + 'liability_understatement'; no clean enum value for 'improper expense/cost-savings recognition.' pre_revelation_quarters_available = 14 (3 10-K + 11 10-Q) filed by the CIK 1637459 entity (post-July-2015 merger) before 2019-02-21; all 14 carry XBRL. Entity's full filing history since 2015 fit in the submissions JSON (no truncation), so 14 is a complete count, not a truncated estimate. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters 14 exact match. Order (a CORRECTED ORDER, caption 'The Kraft Heinz Co.') does not narrate the 2019-02-21 events (write-down/subpoena/drop) -- recorded revelation date rests on contemporaneous market sources; order's only dated public event is the 2019-06-07 restatement 10-K (para.4, 34). $62M penalty confirmed (sec.IV.B).

candidates.revelation_source: Nov 3, 2019 Wall Street Journal report that DOJ and SEC were investigating Under Armour's accounting practices (company confirmed same day; 8-K FD filed Nov 4); https://www.cnbc.com/2019/11/03/under-armour-is-the-subject-of-a-federal-accounting-probe-wsj-reports.html
candidates.scheme_summary: Undisclosed 'pull forward' of ~$408M in existing customer orders over six consecutive quarters (Q3 2015-Q4 2016) to meet analysts' revenue estimates, while attributing revenue growth to other factors and not disclosing the practice or resulting revenue uncertainty in MD&A; SEC did not allege the sales themselves violated GAAP (per SEC AAER-4220 / press release 2021-78).
candidates.notes: AAER order file numbers: 33-10940 / 34-91741 / AAER-4220, Admin Proc 3-20278. Scheme is fundamentally an MD&A/disclosure antifraud case (Sec 17(a)(2)&(3)); SEC expressly did NOT allege the pulled-forward sales failed GAAP, so 'revenue_recognition' tag reflects the underlying revenue-timing practice, not a GAAP-recognition charge. pre_revelation_quarters_available = 14 10-K + 41 10-Q = 55 filed before 2019-11-03 (12 10-K + 33 10-Q from older submissions file 2006-2017, plus 2 10-K + 8 10-Q from recent). Of these, 37 carried XBRL; the earliest 18 (FY2005-FY2009 era, filed 2006-2010) predate XBRL, so xbrl_available is true only for later filings. Investigation actually began (non-public) July 2017 but market did not learn until Nov 3, 2019. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] quarters 55 exact match. Order does not narrate the 2019-11-03 WSJ/investigation disclosure; the market event it describes is 2017-01-31 (Q4-2016 revenue miss, ~23% drop, para.3/36) -- a scheme-consequence event, not the accounting-probe revelation. $9M penalty confirmed (sec.IV.B). GAAP footnote 2 confirmed (no GAAP-violation finding).

candidates.revelation_source: Jan 31, 2020 Muddy Waters Research tweet publicizing an unattributed 89-page short-seller report alleging fabricated sales volumes; https://www.fool.com/investing/2020/01/31/why-luckin-coffee-stock-is-tumbling-today.aspx
candidates.scheme_summary: From at least April 2019 through January 2020, fabricated more than $300 million in retail sales using related parties through three purchasing schemes (per SEC LR-24987/press release 2020-319; Luckin's own Special Committee separately quantified 2019 net-revenue inflation at ~RMB2.12B -- a different measure from a different document), inflated expenses by >$190M, created a fake operations database, and altered accounting/bank records; overstated reported revenue ~28% (period ended 6/30/19) and ~45% (period ended 9/30/19).
candidates.notes: NO AAER was issued — the SEC action is a federal civil injunctive suit, Litigation Release LR-24987 (SEC v. Luckin Coffee, 1:20-cv-10631 SDNY, filed Dec 16 2020), confirmed by the NYU SEED database listing only LR-24987. This strictly fails the 'with an SEC AAER released 2010-2022' inclusion clause even though it is financial-statement fraud by a US-listed ADR issuer; HUMAN MUST DECIDE whether litigation-release-only accounting cases qualify (include set to true on the substance, but flag). Foreign private issuer: filed NO 10-K/10-Q and NO 20-F before the Jan 31 2020 revelation. pre_revelation_quarters_available = 2 counts the quarterly-earnings 6-K furnishings before revelation (Q2 2019 furnished 2019-08-14, accession 0001104659-19-045718; Q3 2019 furnished 2019-11-20, accession 0001104659-19-065621 -- the previously recorded 08-06/11-13 were press-release dates); annual audited financials existed only in the 2019 F-1/424B4 IPO prospectus. No pre-revelation filing carried XBRL (first XBRL was the FY2020 20-F filed 2021-04-15), so xbrl_available=false. Ticker LK on Nasdaq (ADS) until delisting 2020-07-13; now LKNCY on OTC. First public allegation Jan 31 2020 vs company self-disclosure Apr 2 2020 — earliest reported per instruction. [2026-07-03 primary-source recheck] 6-K furnish dates corrected from EDGAR (see above); 4 further non-earnings 6-Ks were filed Jan 2020 pre-revelation. Complaint PDF (comp-pr2020-319.pdf) has corrupted font encoding -- text not machine-extractable; all figures verified against LR-24987 and SEC press release 2020-319 (both sec.gov primary publications). >$300M/>$190M/28%/45%/$180M all confirmed verbatim.
